methodology: Platform fees scraped from each competitor's public pricing page as of Apr 2026. Where fees are tiered (e.g. Eventbrite Essentials vs Professional), the mid-tier rate is used. SG active-organiser counts triangulated from LinkedIn company HQ data, Peatix SG public event counts, Eventbrite SG landing-page event density, and SG venue/experience sector reports (STB 2025, e27, Tech in Asia). Escrow flag = does the platform hold funds until event delivery (vs pay-out-on-sale or T+7). Marketplace flag = is there a genuine 2-sided supply side (vendors list their services) in addition to event listings. sg_localisation_score: +1 each for PayNow/PayLah, SGD-default pricing, SG GST/IRAS invoice-ready, SG phone/CS support, and Singlish/Mandarin/Malay/Tamil UI. Estimates are flagged in pricing_flag.

categories.global_ticketing: Large international ticketing platforms (Eventbrite, Ticketmaster, Universe, Humanitix, Xing Events)
categories.sea_ticketing: Southeast Asia / Singapore local ticketing platforms (Peatix, SISTIC, EventNook, Ticketbooth, EventShopSG)
categories.experience_marketplace: Curated attraction/tour/experience marketplaces (Klook, KKday, GetYourGuide, Airbnb Experiences)
categories.space_rental: Venue and space rental marketplaces (Peerspace, Splacer, Giggster, Venuerific, Venuescape)
categories.class_workshop: Class, cohort-course and workshop platforms (Skillshare, CreativeLive, Thinkific, Eatwith, FunEmpire)
categories.freelance_marketplace: Freelance / gig marketplaces used for event support vendors (Fiverr, Upwork, Bark, GetYourGig, SGHustle)
categories.community_newsletter: Community-led, meetup or newsletter-driven event discovery (Meetup, BYOB SG, SG Ladies)
categories.payment_escrow: Social payment, group-pay and generic escrow services (PayNow, Revolut, Venmo, Escrow.com)
categories.diy_alt: The silent-majority DIY stack (Google Form + PayNow + WhatsApp group)

competitors.name: Eventbrite
competitors.target_market: SG semi-pro organisers running 50-500 seat paid events (conferences, classes, nightlife) who want global discovery
competitors.pricing_notes: Flex: 3.7% + SGD 1.59/ticket service fee + 3.5% payment processing = ~10.7% all-in on a SGD 50 ticket. Pro tier adds SGD 15/ticket cap. Free events are free.
competitors.primary_value_prop: The default global event discovery and ticketing platform with built-in SEO and email marketing.
strengths: Massive organic discovery via eventbrite.sg domain authority
Mature organiser tools: recurring events, promo codes, waitlists, check-in app
Integrates with Mailchimp, HubSpot, Salesforce out of box
weaknesses: Funds paid out T+4-7 business days AFTER event (not true escrow - buyer has no recourse if organiser cancels)
Fees compounded on SGD 20-40 tickets make it expensive for small workshops
No vendor/freelancer marketplace - organisers must source photographers/caterers elsewhere

competitors.name: Ticketmaster
competitors.target_market: Enterprise promoters and venues running 1000+ seat concerts, sports, large-format entertainment in SG
competitors.pricing_notes: Combined service + facility + processing fees on attendee side typically 15-25% of face value; organiser-side commercial terms are negotiated and hidden.
competitors.primary_value_prop: The stadium-scale ticketing incumbent with the biggest inventory for concerts and sports in SG.
strengths: Exclusive deals with National Stadium, Indoor Stadium, major promoters
Anti-scalping and verified-fan tech that small platforms cannot match
Massive consumer brand recognition for ticket buyers
weaknesses: Not available self-serve - zero long-tail creator use
No experiences, no classes, no marketplace of support vendors

competitors.name: Universe (by Ticketmaster)
competitors.target_market: Mid-market North American event creators (5k-50k attendees/year) running ticketed experiences; minor SG footprint
competitors.pricing_notes: 2% + USD 0.99 + 3% payment = roughly 7-8% on a USD 40 ticket. Free events free.
competitors.primary_value_prop: A lower-fee, more creator-friendly sibling of Ticketmaster aimed at self-serve organisers.
strengths: Cheaper than Eventbrite for mid-price tickets
Owned by Ticketmaster so connects to some TM inventory

competitors.name: Humanitix
competitors.target_market: Purpose-driven and nonprofit organisers in ANZ / SG running community, education and charity events
competitors.pricing_notes: 1.99% + SGD 0.99/ticket booking fee + ~3% payment processing; all profits donated to children's charities. Free events pay only processing.
competitors.primary_value_prop: The not-for-profit Eventbrite - cheaper fees, all platform profit goes to charity.
strengths: Genuinely lower fees than Eventbrite for mission-driven orgs
100% of booking-fee profit donated - strong PR narrative
Seating maps, donation tools, accessibility features built-in
weaknesses: Low SG brand awareness outside charity/education circles
Still not escrow - payout is on ticket sale, not post-event

competitors.name: XING Events / Amiando
competitors.target_market: European B2B conference organisers; negligible SG presence
competitors.pricing_notes: 5.9% + EUR 0.99/ticket service fee, plus payment processing ~2%. Enterprise flat-fee contracts also available.
competitors.primary_value_prop: Europe's B2B conference ticketing engine with deep CRM integrations.
strengths: Strong enterprise conference tooling (badge printing, lead scanning)
Integrates with XING / LinkedIn for B2B discovery
weaknesses: Pure ticketing - no escrow or marketplace

competitors.name: Peatix (SG)
competitors.target_market: SG indie creators, community organisers and small businesses running SGD 10-60 tickets for meetups, workshops, food pop-ups
competitors.pricing_notes: 4.9% + SGD 0.99/paid ticket service fee + 2.5-3% payment processing. Free events are free.
competitors.primary_value_prop: The de-facto community ticketing platform for SG indie events with a mobile-first discovery app.
strengths: Strongest SG indie-event brand after Eventbrite (Peatix SG has ~400+ active monthly organisers)
Mobile app with push notifications and follow-organiser feed
Clean flat fee structure, PayNow available
weaknesses: Funds paid on sale, not after event - no buyer protection if organiser ghosts
Discovery is fading vs Instagram/TikTok native event promotion

competitors.name: Peatix Japan
competitors.target_market: Japanese community and small-business event organisers (comparison benchmark for SG)
competitors.pricing_notes: 4.9% + JPY 99/ticket + 3% payment processing, functionally identical to SG pricing.
competitors.primary_value_prop: Japan's dominant indie-community ticketing platform - the original mothership.
strengths: 10+ years of product maturity in indie events
Stronger Japanese community loyalty than SG
Proof-point for the grown-up version of Peatix SG
weaknesses: Same structural issues: no escrow, no marketplace

competitors.name: SISTIC
competitors.target_market: SG arts, theatre, sports, concert promoters (NAC-backed venues, Esplanade, Kallang Wave, etc.)
competitors.pricing_notes: Attendee-side booking fee ~SGD 3-4/ticket + SGD 1 handling. Organiser-side commissions 4-10% negotiated, enterprise only.
competitors.primary_value_prop: Singapore's national ticketing agency for arts, sports and large concerts with Esplanade/SINDA/NAC tie-ins.
strengths: Near-monopoly on major SG arts and sports venues

competitors.name: EventNook
competitors.target_market: SG corporate conferences, seminars, trade shows (50-2000 pax B2B events with registration flows)
competitors.pricing_notes: 1.5% + SGD 0.50/ticket + payment processing ~2.9%. Free plan available for free events. Enterprise flat-fee white-label tier.
competitors.primary_value_prop: SG-built, self-serve event registration and ticketing for corporate and professional events.
strengths: Local SG company, SGD-native, PayNow supported
Strong badge printing + on-site check-in for conferences
Lower fees than Eventbrite for B2B use cases
weaknesses: No escrow - same T+ payout structure
No vendor marketplace; weak social/community features

competitors.name: Ticketbooth
competitors.target_market: SG mid-market concert promoters, nightlife, niche festivals wanting a SISTIC alternative
competitors.pricing_notes: Estimated 3% + SGD 1/ticket + 2.5% payment processing; commercial rates negotiated, not fully published.
competitors.primary_value_prop: A nimbler SG-local alternative to SISTIC for concerts, clubs and mid-size festivals.
weaknesses: Small audience base - organisers must drive all traffic
Thin brand moat vs SISTIC above and Peatix below

competitors.name: EventShopSG
competitors.target_market: SG small-business and community organisers doing charity dinners, school events, association D&Ds
competitors.pricing_notes: Advertised 'zero platform fee' for free events; 3-4% + SGD 0.50 for paid. Partial disclosure.
competitors.primary_value_prop: Low-fee SG ticketing for community, school and charity events.
strengths: Very low fees attractive to charities/schools

competitors.name: Klook
competitors.target_market: Tourist and local leisure consumers booking pre-packaged attractions, tours and day experiences in SG and regionally
competitors.pricing_notes: Supplier commission typically 20-25% of listed price, negotiated; payout on redemption (effective escrow for providers).
competitors.primary_value_prop: Asia's leading experience marketplace - pre-packaged attractions, tours and activities with instant mobile redemption.
strengths: Massive SG tourist + local consumer traffic (>2M SG MAU)
weaknesses: Curated onboarding - small creators cannot self-list
Zero B2B / community / corporate event support

competitors.name: KKday
competitors.target_market: Regional leisure travellers booking curated Asia experiences, day tours and workshops
competitors.pricing_notes: Supplier commission 20-25%; B2B2C tier for travel agents. Not publicly posted.
competitors.primary_value_prop: Taiwan-HQed experience marketplace strong in Japan/Taiwan with growing SG presence.
weaknesses: Smaller SG consumer base than Klook
Curated-only; not open to indie creators
Heavy discounting culture erodes creator margins

competitors.name: GetYourGuide
competitors.target_market: Inbound tourists to SG booking curated tours and attractions
competitors.pricing_notes: Supplier commission 20-30%, typical 25%. Not publicly posted.
competitors.primary_value_prop: Europe-HQed experience marketplace with the strongest inbound-tourist funnel globally.
strengths: Best-in-class Google search SEO for attractions
weaknesses: Tiny share of local SG experiences

competitors.name: Airbnb Experiences
competitors.target_market: SG hosts running 2-15 pax curated experiences (cooking, art, tours) for tourists and locals
competitors.pricing_notes: 20% host service fee on experience price; payout 24 hours after experience starts (quasi-escrow).
competitors.primary_value_prop: The global brand for small-group hosted experiences with instant booking and tourist trust.
strengths: Huge inbound tourist audience with existing Airbnb account
High trust + review graph carries over from stays
weaknesses: Relaunched and scaled back multiple times - inventory in SG is thin
Strict curation; not self-serve for most creators

competitors.name: Peerspace
competitors.target_market: North American creatives booking hourly spaces for shoots, offsites, events (limited SG inventory)
competitors.pricing_notes: 15% host service fee + 5% guest service fee + payment processing.
competitors.primary_value_prop: Airbnb for production spaces - book hourly venues for events, film, photo.
strengths: Best-in-class UX for hourly space booking
weaknesses: No ticketing or event sales layer

competitors.name: Splacer
competitors.target_market: US/EU event producers booking boutique venues; effectively no SG presence
competitors.pricing_notes: 15% host commission + 6% booker fee + Stripe processing.
competitors.primary_value_prop: Curated design-led venue marketplace for boutique events.

competitors.name: Giggster
competitors.target_market: Film/photo shoot producers booking hourly locations in North America
competitors.pricing_notes: 15% host service fee + guest service fee ~5% + Stripe.
competitors.primary_value_prop: Production-focused space marketplace with insurance bundled.

competitors.name: Venuerific
competitors.target_market: SG corporate and social organisers booking venues for weddings, D&Ds, parties, workshops (100-800 pax)
competitors.pricing_notes: 10-15% commission on confirmed bookings (negotiated with venues). Lead-gen model; venue directly invoices client.
competitors.primary_value_prop: SEA's leading venue-discovery marketplace - 6000+ SG venues listed.
weaknesses: Pure lead-gen - no escrow, no ticketing
Commission opacity; many venues quote outside the platform

competitors.name: Venuescape
competitors.target_market: SG corporate event planners booking meeting rooms, offsite venues, workshops
competitors.pricing_notes: 10% booking commission; private enterprise quotes.
competitors.primary_value_prop: SG-local venue-booking platform focused on corporate offsites and workshops.

competitors.name: Skillshare
competitors.target_market: Global online creators publishing async video classes in design, business, productivity
competitors.pricing_notes: Student pays SGD 19/month subscription; teacher paid per-minute-watched from royalty pool. No per-transaction fee for teachers.
competitors.primary_value_prop: Subscription-based on-demand class library for creative skills.
strengths: Passive earnings once class is produced
weaknesses: Irrelevant for paid live events with escrow

competitors.name: CreativeLive
competitors.target_market: Pro photographers, musicians, designers buying premium pre-recorded and livestream classes
competitors.pricing_notes: Curated-instructor model; ~50/50 revenue share. Not self-serve.
competitors.primary_value_prop: Premium creative education with curated celebrity instructors.
weaknesses: Not relevant for SG live events

competitors.name: Thinkific
competitors.target_market: Independent course creators running cohort-based and async courses on their own domain
competitors.pricing_notes: Basic SGD 49/mo, Pro SGD 99/mo, Premier SGD 499/mo; 0% transaction fee on paid plans.
competitors.primary_value_prop: White-label course platform for creators who want to own their audience and brand.
strengths: Zero transaction fees on paid plans
weaknesses: Flat monthly fee punishing for <10 sales/mo organisers
Zero discovery - creator must drive all traffic
No escrow, no local payment, no vendor marketplace

competitors.name: Eatwith
competitors.target_market: Home-dining hosts running small-group supper clubs and cooking classes for travellers
competitors.pricing_notes: 15-20% host commission; payout after experience = escrow-like.
competitors.primary_value_prop: Airbnb for food experiences - supper clubs and home cooking classes.

competitors.name: FunEmpire
competitors.target_market: SG corporate HR and team-building buyers booking workshops and experiences for company offsites
competitors.pricing_notes: Agency-margin model; effective ~25-35% markup between provider cost and buyer price. Not disclosed as 'fees'.
competitors.primary_value_prop: SG's largest team-building and corporate-experience aggregator.
strengths: Dominates corporate-HR buyer channel in SG

competitors.name: Fiverr (Events category)
competitors.target_market: Global buyers sourcing freelance event support (MCs, virtual event ops, video editors) at productised price points
competitors.pricing_notes: 20% seller commission + 5.5% + USD 2.50 buyer fee.
competitors.primary_value_prop: Productised freelance services gig marketplace with fixed packages.
weaknesses: Race-to-bottom pricing hurts SG premium providers

competitors.name: Upwork
competitors.target_market: Global companies and agencies hiring hourly / project freelancers incl. event producers, designers, videographers
competitors.pricing_notes: 10% flat freelancer fee + 5% buyer marketplace fee + Connects bidding cost.
competitors.primary_value_prop: The mature global hourly-contract freelance marketplace with robust escrow.
weaknesses: Not event-native - no ticketing or discovery
Poor fit for same-day SG event gigs

competitors.name: Bark.com
competitors.target_market: SG consumers seeking local service quotes - photographers, DJs, caterers, MCs
competitors.pricing_notes: Pay-per-lead to pros (SGD 20-80 per quote credit); buyers free. Pros spend SGD 100-500/month on credits.
competitors.primary_value_prop: Lead-generation marketplace for local service pros - pros pay per lead.
strengths: Active SG category pros across event services
weaknesses: Pay-per-lead model burns pro budgets with low conversion

competitors.name: GetYourGig
competitors.target_market: SG event organisers sourcing musicians, MCs, magicians, lion-dance and emcee talent
competitors.pricing_notes: 15% booking commission on confirmed gigs; payout on completion.
competitors.primary_value_prop: SG-local performer marketplace for weddings, corporate and private events.
strengths: SG-local talent focus - musicians, MCs, lion dance
Some booking escrow on confirmed gigs
weaknesses: Narrow to performers only - no photographers/caterers

competitors.name: SGHustle
competitors.target_market: SG side-hustlers and small gig providers (photography, tutoring, event staffing, handyman)
competitors.pricing_notes: ~10% service fee on completed bookings; limited public disclosure.
competitors.primary_value_prop: SG gig-economy marketplace for side-hustlers across services.

competitors.name: Meetup.com
competitors.target_market: Community and special-interest group organisers running recurring free/low-cost meetups
competitors.pricing_notes: Organiser subscription SGD 22-33/month (Basic/Unlimited). Free for members.
competitors.primary_value_prop: The original community-meetup platform with strong recurring-group network effects.
strengths: Persistent SG community groups (tech, yoga, book clubs)
weaknesses: Not a ticketing platform - paid event flow is clunky
Steady decline in SG activity post-pandemic

competitors.name: BYOB SG
competitors.target_market: SG professional women 28-45 attending curated socials, dinners and mini-retreats
competitors.pricing_notes: Direct-sold via newsletter + DM; payment usually PayNow or Stripe on own site. No platform fee.
competitors.primary_value_prop: SG curated community for professional women, sold via newsletter and IG.
weaknesses: No escrow - attendees bear refund risk

competitors.name: SG Ladies (community)
competitors.target_market: SG women buying/selling tickets, workshops and services within a 100k+ member FB group
competitors.pricing_notes: Free discovery via FB group; transactions happen off-platform (PayNow).
competitors.primary_value_prop: SG's largest women's FB community - a de-facto free marketplace for tickets and workshops.
weaknesses: No escrow - scam and no-show risk is real
No event tooling, pure social post

competitors.name: PayNow (group transfer)
competitors.target_market: Every SG adult - the universal P2P rail for collecting payments for informal events
competitors.pricing_notes: Free to send and receive between SG banks via NRIC/UEN/mobile.
competitors.primary_value_prop: The universal, free SG P2P money rail that underpins most DIY event collection.
strengths: 100% of SG banked adults have it
weaknesses: Not escrow - once sent, funds are gone

competitors.name: Revolut (Pots / Group Bills)
competitors.target_market: SG digital-native users splitting group bills, multi-currency travel pots
competitors.pricing_notes: Free Standard tier; Premium SGD 9.99/mo. Core group-bills and pots features free.
competitors.primary_value_prop: Multi-currency wallet with group pots used to collect for travel and events.
weaknesses: Not escrow - host controls the pot

competitors.name: Venmo / Cash App
competitors.target_market: US consumers for P2P; effectively zero SG usage, listed for taxonomy completeness
competitors.pricing_notes: Free P2P for bank-funded transfers; 3% for credit-card funded.
competitors.primary_value_prop: US P2P payment dominant equivalent of PayNow.

competitors.name: Escrow.com
competitors.target_market: High-ticket transactional buyers/sellers (domains, vehicles, art) needing formal escrow
competitors.pricing_notes: 0.89%-3.25% sliding by transaction size; minimum USD 10 per escrow.
competitors.primary_value_prop: Regulated third-party escrow for one-off high-ticket transactions.
strengths: True licensed escrow with dispute resolution
weaknesses: Way too heavy / slow for SGD 40 event tickets

competitors.name: DIY Stack (Google Form + PayNow + WhatsApp)
competitors.target_market: The silent majority of SG hobby-to-business creators running sub-100-seat paid workshops, classes and socials
competitors.pricing_notes: Free: Google Form for RSVPs, PayNow for collection, WhatsApp group for comms, Linktree for discovery.
competitors.primary_value_prop: The zero-fee, zero-learning-curve DIY stack that every SG organiser already uses.
strengths: Zero fees - 100% of revenue to organiser
Every SG attendee already knows the tools
weaknesses: No escrow - attendees lose money if organiser ghosts
No discovery - every event starts from zero
No analytics, no vendor marketplace, no GST invoicing
